TREC 2007 Spam Track. We are still in the process of finalizing the TREC 2007 Spam Track guidelines. This is an interim report. We should have the final guidelines by the end of June. In the meantime, please feel free to post queries or comments to this list. The primary tasks will use the same tool kit and data format as last year; i.e. the TREC spam filter evaluation toolkit available here: http://plg.uwaterloo.ca/~gvcormac/spam/ The active learning task will use a different version of the toolkit, to be available shortly. The three tasks are: On-line filtering with immediate feedback. - exactly the same task as for TREC 2005 and 2006 On-line filtering with delayed/incomplete/noisy feedback. - same tools as for the TREC 2006 delayted feedback, but the test data may not contain "train" commands for every message, and some of the "train" commands may be wrong so as to simulate user underreporting and user error. Emphasis will be placed on correct classification of a large number of messages with no feedback. (For example, there may be no feedback for the last half of the messages) On-line active filtering with active learning. - different tools and task from TREC 2006 active learning Filters will perform on-line classification as for the other two tasks, but will be allowed to query the true class (ham or spam) of a fraction of the messages, chosen by the filter. This will be effected by an additional command "query" added to the toolkit: query where is a message previously classified by the filter. Filters will be submitted to NIST (date to be determined, but probably mid-July) after which a public corpus will be released. Results on the public corpus will also be submitted to NIST (probably late August). -- Gordon V. Cormack CS Dept, University of Waterloo, Canada N2L 3G1 qcdjhryli@uwaterloo.ca http://cormack.uwaterloo.ca/cormack

Podcast update: Threats and trends for 2008. Update February 08, 2008 Published by SearchSecurity.com Update THIS ISSUE: > From the Editor: Podcast update: Threats and trends for 2008 FROM THE EDITOR Podcast update: Threats and trends for 2008 [Maggie Wright, Assistant Editor] rybamlk@techtarget.com Per usual, we have several new and interesting audio downloads for you this month, highlighting topics such as malware, network access control and the threat landscape for 2008. Malware has been a hot topic in '08. Nugache, sophisticated rootkits and ransomware have all made headlines. Now, after quietly falling off the radar, PDF spam could be making a comeback. Last week, MX Logic, an antispam and managed services vendor, reported that it is tracking PDF spam found in unwanted messages. As part of our Seacurity Wire Weekly podcast series, Sam Masiello, director of threat management at MX Logic, offered his opinions on why the format has resurfaced, how security researchers found it and whether an increase in PDF spam is likely. Also, security researchers at Facetime Communications warn of a new do-it-yourself phishing program that assists phishers in attacking social networking sites like MySpace and Facebook. The network access control (NAC) market was the topic of choice in our Jan. 23rd Security Wire Weekly edition. Dave Bixler, CISO for Siemens IT Solutions and Services, talks about the state of the network access control market, where NAC technology is headed and why he thinks it's smart for organizations to hold off on NAC deployments. In our first Security Squad podcast of the New Year, the SearchSecurity.com editors discussed the likelihood of a massive cyberattack in 2008. The team also examines the Sears spyware scandal. In last month's Threat Monitor podcast, security threats expert Ed Skoudis unveiled what he thinks will be the biggest security threats in 2008, and offers advice on how corporations can defend against them. Some of the threats mentioned include spear-phishing, botnets and malware email attacks. Be sure to check out our brand-new Compliance School podcast, Fact or fiction: Involving end users in the compliance program. Richard E. Mackey, vice president of consultancy SystemExperts, discloses the tools needed to educate end users about the importance of compliance, how users can get involved in the compliance program and why compliance is an essential aspect of corporate risk management. We're adding new recordings all the time, so remember, you can visit our audio downloads page at any time to listen to all of our security podcasts and subscribe to our podcast feeds. Have a great weekend! SITE HIGHLIGHTS Info center: Information security governance Ensure that your organization's tools, people and business processes meet all of your information security needs. PODCASTS & WEBCASTS Webcast: Best practices for database security and compliance WHEN: Feb. 13th, 2:00 p.m. ET (19:00 GMT) SPEAKERS: Noel Yuhanna, Principal Analyst Phil Neray, v.p. of Marketing, Guardium SPONSOR: Guardium, Inc. Thank you for your kindness and generosity. Warm regards, Sarah Harris Refugee Support Coordinator

Dear Isabella, With the rollout of COVID-19 vaccines, there is hope on the horizon for ending the pandemic. Here's what you need to know about the COVID-19 vaccine: - Safety and efficacy: Extensive clinical trials have shown that COVID-19 vaccines are safe and effective at preventing severe illness and reducing the spread of the virus. - Vaccine availability: Vaccines are being distributed in phases based on priority groups, such as healthcare workers, essential workers, and high-risk individuals. Availability may vary depending on your location and eligibility criteria. - Vaccine administration: Vaccines are administered in two doses, spaced several weeks apart, depending on the manufacturer. Follow-up appointments are necessary to ensure full vaccination. - Side effects: Mild side effects such as soreness at the injection site, fatigue, and mild fever are common and typically resolve within a few days. Serious side effects are rare. - Continued precautions: While vaccination is an essential tool in controlling the pandemic, it's crucial to continue following safety guidelines such as wearing masks and practicing social distancing until herd immunity is achieved. For more information about COVID-19 vaccines, visit the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) website or consult with your healthcare provider. Stay informed and stay safe, Sophia Brown Public Health Educator [Company Name]

[UAI] Job Opening at BISC Program, UCB;. ********************************************************************* Berkeley Initiative in Soft Computing (BISC) ********************************************************************* ********************************************************************* Please read the qualification for Jobs before you Apply. Incomplete applications will not be considered for review. The current opening positions at BISC are: ------------------------------------------------------------------- -- Software Developer and R&D for the BISC-DSS (Decision Support System) [Ref. Dr. Nikravesh, BISC-DSS and Web-Based DSS) -- Software Developer and R&D for the BISC - Q&A System (Question & Answering System)[Ref. Prof. Zadeh's Recent work; From Search Engine to Question Answering System] For more information about BISC-DSS and Q&A System please visit BISC website. ========================================================= ********************************************************** Applicant are invited for several postdoctoral/ research professional positions at BISC Program-University of California, Berkeley. You'll enjoy challenging projects and a unique work environment at BISC Program. The BISC program carry out world class research and advanced development activities in area of Soft Computing and Artificial Intelligence. Research teams within BISC Program-UCB take part in international R&D programmes to ensure that the research remains leading edge and relevant to the BISC's strategic objectives. BISC Program have been successfully exploiting results of its research in the sponsors companies' project creating significant values for our partners. The successful candidates will have the opportunity to be involved in defining new research field/directions and will take an active role in carrying out R&D activities in an area that will be of significant importance for BISC future. Required Qualifications and skills : ============================ We are seeking recognized researchers to play active roles in BISC program. - --They should have PhD or equivalent degree in areas such as computer science and/or Engineering field, artificial intelligence, and/or soft computing with emphasis to one or more of the following 1) the Search Engine and Query, 2) Question-Answering System, 3) Decision Support Systems, Time Series Analysis/Signal Processing. - -- They must have good software engineering skills and practical experience in Internet based technology and software development, Java, C++ and systems programming. - -- They should have an excellent publication record track in refereed journals and good communication skills. Salary: Depends on Experience and Qualification PostDocs: $40,512-$55,668 Research Positions: $53,900-$71,400 Others: Open Date for Accepting Applications: August 2004 Closing Date: Nov. 2004 Review process Begins: September 2004 Starting Date: Oct. 2004-Jan 2005 Please forward your CV and supporting information including copy of three published papers in refereed journals (relevant to the position and the field that you are applying for), and cover letter to: M. Nikravesh BISC Associate Director 199MF Cory Hall EECS Department University of Calaifornia-Berkeley Berkeley, CA 94720 Or Email electronic copy to: gmoizjagn@eecs.berkeley.edu in Subject (Applying for Position at BISC Program) ================================================ ================================================ ****************************************************** -- How to Join BISC Program: --------------------------------- http://www-bisc.cs.berkeley.edu/BISCProgram/Sponsors.htm ----------------------------------------------------------------- **************************************************** -- BISC Associate Program ------------------------------- http://www-bisc.cs.berkeley.edu/BISCProgram/Associate.htm ----------------------------------------------------------------- **************************************************** -------------------------------------------------------------------- If you ever want to remove yourself from this mailing list, you can send mail to with the following command in the body of your email message: unsubscribe bisc-group or from another account, unsubscribe bisc-group

Please consider making a contribution today to support our military families in their time of need. You can donate securely online at [Link: https://military-family-relief.org]. Thank you for standing with our military families during this challenging time. Warm regards, David Wilson Military Family Support Fund

Re: [opensuse] SUSE 10.3 + ATI FireGL 5250 + Latest ATI driver = no 3D efects. AV wrote: > On Feb 9, 2008 9:24 AM, David C. Rankin wrote: > >> Start by posting /etc/X11/xorg.conf, /var/log/Xorg.0.log, and the >> output of: >> >> (1) glxinfo; run from a terminal; >> (2) rpm -qa | grep fglrx; also run from a console > > Here it is all files. I am using KDE desktop. > > Audrius > x11-video-fglrxG01-8.45.4-0.2 ati-fglrxG01-kmp-default-8.45.4_2.6.22.16_0.1-0.2 Fine. Xorg.0.log: (**) Option "AIGLX" "on" Good, indirect rendering is enabled, the rest is fine. xorg.conf: Fine. All in all your config looks fine. There doesn't appear to be anything that would prevent compiz from loading. 3 Things: (1) Copy the following into a script and run it from the command line to try and start compiz: #!/bin/bash emerald --replace & sleep 1 LIBGL_ALWAYS_INDIRECT=1 compiz \\ --ignore-desktop-hints \\ --replace \\ --indirect-rendering \\ --no-libgl-fallback \\ --sm-disable \\ ccp & or just enter the following from a command line. Both commands are "single" line commands but they get wrapped below. If it works, you can modify your fusion-icon scripts to include the needed command line options. emerald --replace & LIBGL_ALWAYS_INDIRECT=1 compiz --ignore-desktop-hints --replace --indirect-rendering --no-libgl-fallback --sm-disable ccp & [you can ignore the hundreds of messages that get dumped back to the terminal if compiz starts. If it doesn't start and if there are only a handful of error, they may be useful] (2) if it doesn't, can you run fgl_glxgears from a terminal? (3) if still having problems, make sure your compiz files are updated by using yast->software management->actions->all packages->update if newer package is available You will probably have to choose "ignore conflicts here" for the compiz conflicts in the X11/XGL repository. Then run "rpm -qa | grep compiz" from a console and post the output. Good luck! -- David C. Rankin, J.D., P.E. Dear Mr. Christopher Wilson, I hope this email finds you in good health. As tax season unfolds, I wanted to offer some expert advice to help you unlock the full potential of your tax refund. One strategy to consider is maximizing your retirement contributions. Contributing to retirement accounts such as IRAs or 401(k)s not only reduces your taxable income but also enhances your long-term financial security. By saving for retirement, you're not only lowering your tax bill but also investing in your future. Another overlooked area is capitalizing on eligible tax deductions. Whether it's medical expenses, mortgage interest, or business-related expenses, every deductible item can lower your taxable income and increase your refund amount. Be sure to keep meticulous records and consult with a tax professional to ensure you're claiming all available deductions. Lastly, don't overlook tax-saving opportunities such as health savings accounts (HSAs) or education-related tax benefits. These accounts and credits can further reduce your tax liability and amplify your refund potential. If you'd like personalized assistance with your taxes or have specific questions, please don't hesitate to reach out to me at [jennifer@martinezcpanetwork.com]. Together, we can devise a tax strategy tailored to your unique financial situation. Thank you for considering my advice. Here's to a prosperous tax season and a substantial refund! Warm regards, Jennifer Martinez Certified Public Accountant (CPA)

Dear Mr. Steven Anderson, I hope this email finds you well. My name is Rachel Peterson, and I represent the Clean Water Initiative. In conflict-affected regions, access to clean water and sanitation is often compromised, leaving communities vulnerable to waterborne diseases and contamination. The lack of basic necessities exacerbates the humanitarian crisis and threatens the health and well-being of countless individuals. Your support can provide access to clean water, sanitation facilities, and hygiene education to war-affected communities, helping prevent the spread of disease and ensuring the health and dignity of those in need. Please consider making a donation today to support our clean water initiatives in conflict zones. You can donate securely online at [Link: https://clean-water-initiative.org]. Thank you for your compassion and generosity in helping us provide lifesaving aid to those affected by war. Best regards, Rachel Peterson Clean Water Initiative

Dear Ms. Samantha Harris, I hope this email finds you in good health. With tax season upon us, I wanted to share some important steps to help you secure your tax refund and minimize potential delays. First and foremost, ensure that your tax return is accurate and complete. Double-check all information, including Social Security numbers, income sources, and deductions, to avoid errors that could delay your refund. Consider filing your taxes electronically and opting for direct deposit of your refund. Electronic filing is faster, more secure, and reduces the risk of errors compared to paper filing. Direct deposit ensures that your refund reaches you promptly, without the need for paper checks. If you're expecting a refund, file your taxes early to expedite the process. Filing early reduces the likelihood of processing delays and ensures that you receive your refund as soon as possible. Lastly, be vigilant against tax-related scams and phishing attempts. The IRS will never initiate contact via email, text message, or social media, so be wary of unsolicited communication requesting personal or financial information. If you have any questions or need assistance with your taxes, please feel free to reach out to me at [christopher@taylorcpa.com]. I'm here to help you navigate the tax landscape and secure the refund you deserve. Thank you for entrusting me with your tax matters. Here's to a successful tax season and a generous refund! Best regards, Christopher Taylor Certified Public Accountant (CPA)

Dear Matthew, I hope this email finds you in good spirits. We are thrilled to extend to you an invitation to our annual charity auction benefiting cancer research initiatives. The charity auction will be held on January 15th at 7:00 PM at the luxurious Grand Plaza. It promises to be an evening filled with elegance, excitement, and the opportunity to make a meaningful impact. To view our auction catalog and reserve your seat, please visit our event page [here](https://charity-event-link11.com). Your consistent support has been instrumental in providing hope and healing to cancer patients and their families, Matthew. We invite you to join us once again as we work towards a future free from cancer. Thank you for your unwavering commitment to our cause. We look forward to seeing you at the auction. Best regards, Erica Miller Charity Auction Coordinator

Author: jerry Date: 2007-07-05 19:55:40 +0000 (Thu, 05 Jul 2007) New Revision: 23730 WebSVN: http://websvn.samba.org/cgi-bin/viewcvs.cgi?view=rev&root=samba&rev=23730 Log: Squashed commit of the following: commit 3941269fa01038fca242a197e8d7c1f234d45ea7 Author: Gerald (Jerry) Carter Date: Thu Jul 5 14:52:03 2007 -0500 Two fixes for "winbind expand groups". (a) Update the counter for the number of new groups to resolve else we'll only expand one group member per level and drop the rest. (b) Don't reset the num_names counter in winbindd_ads.c:lookup_groupmem() or we'll drop the SIDs resolved to names via cache from the resulting list. Modified: branches/SAMBA_3_0/source/nsswitch/winbindd_ads.c branches/SAMBA_3_0/source/nsswitch/winbindd_group.c Changeset: Modified: branches/SAMBA_3_0/source/nsswitch/winbindd_ads.c =================================================================== --- branches/SAMBA_3_0/source/nsswitch/winbindd_ads.c 2007-07-05 19:55:25 UTC (rev 23729) +++ branches/SAMBA_3_0/source/nsswitch/winbindd_ads.c 2007-07-05 19:55:40 UTC (rev 23730) @@ -1017,7 +1017,11 @@ DEBUG(10,("ads: lookup_groupmem: got sid %s from cache\\n", sid_string_static(&sid))); sid_copy(&(*sid_mem)[*num_names], &sid); - (*names)[*num_names] = CONST_DISCARD(char *,name); + (*names)[*num_names] = talloc_asprintf(*names, "%s%c%s", + domain_name, + *lp_winbind_separator(), + name ); + (*name_types)[*num_names] = name_type; (*num_names)++; } @@ -1055,7 +1059,6 @@ /* Copy the entries over from the "_nocache" arrays * to the result arrays, skipping the gaps the * lookup_sids call left. */ - *num_names = 0; for (i=0; i < num_nocache; i++) { if (((names_nocache)[i] != NULL) && ((name_types_nocache)[i] != SID_NAME_UNKNOWN)) Modified: branches/SAMBA_3_0/source/nsswitch/winbindd_group.c =================================================================== --- branches/SAMBA_3_0/source/nsswitch/winbindd_group.c 2007-07-05 19:55:25 UTC (rev 23729) +++ branches/SAMBA_3_0/source/nsswitch/winbindd_group.c 2007-07-05 19:55:40 UTC (rev 23730) @@ -576,6 +576,7 @@ /* One more round */ TALLOC_FREE(glist); glist = new_glist; + n_glist = n_new_glist; } TALLOC_FREE( glist );

Dear Mr. Kevin Parker, I hope this email finds you present and centered. In today's fast-paced world, mindfulness offers a sanctuary of peace and tranquility amidst the chaos. As someone who values mental well-being, I invite you to explore the transformative practice of mindfulness with us at the Center for Mindfulness and Well-Being. Through guided meditation sessions, mindfulness workshops, and mindful living courses, you can cultivate a deeper sense of awareness, compassion, and inner peace. Whether you're new to mindfulness or seeking to deepen your practice, our organization offers a supportive community where you can embark on a journey of self-discovery and personal growth. To learn more about our mindfulness programs and upcoming events, please visit our website: [https://unlock-mindfulness-laura.com] Thank you for your commitment to well-being, and I look forward to cultivating mindfulness together. Warm regards, Laura Turner Wellness Advocate Center for Mindfulness and Well-Being
